heading: Near real-time monitoring and mitigation program for large cetaceans
label: 1391 Near real-time monitoring and mitigation program for large cetaceans
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Don Young Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2022 and also as part of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, and not as part of the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 which comprises this chapter.

DefinitionsFor definition of “Secretary” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under , Coast Guard.section 11002 of div. K of Pub. L. 117–263section 106 of Title 14For definitions of terms used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 11301 of div. K of Pub. L. 117–263section 1390 of this title

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Under Secretary, in coordination with the heads of other relevant Federal agencies, shall design and deploy a cost-effective, efficient, and results-oriented near real-time monitoring and mitigation program (referred to in this section as the “Program”) for threatened or endangered cetaceans.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: b Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the Program shall be to reduce the risk to large cetaceans posed by vessel collisions and to minimize other impacts on large cetaceans through the use of near real-time location monitoring and location information.
prioritize species of large cetaceans for which impacts from vessel collisions are of particular concern;
prioritize areas where such impacts are of particular concern;

be capable of detecting and alerting ocean users and enforcement agencies of the probable location of large cetaceans on an actionable real-time basis, including through real-time data whenever possible;
inform sector-specific mitigation protocols to effectively reduce takes (as defined in section 216.3 of title 50, Code of Federal Regulations, or successor regulations) of large cetaceans;
integrate technology improvements; and
be informed by technologies, monitoring methods, and mitigation protocols developed under the pilot project required under subsection (d).

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: In carrying out the Program, the Under Secretary shall first establish a pilot monitoring and mitigation project (referred to in this section as the “pilot project”) for North Atlantic right whales for the purposes of informing the Program.

children.label: A
children.text: core foraging habitats; and

children.label: B
children.text: important feeding, breeding, calving, rearing, or migratory habitats of North Atlantic right whales that co-occur with areas of high risk of mortality or serious injury of such whales from vessels, vessel strikes, or disturbance.

children.label: A
children.text: comprises the best available detection power, spatial coverage, and survey effort to detect and localize North Atlantic right whales within habitats described in paragraph (2);

children.label: B
children.text: is capable of detecting North Atlantic right whales, including visually and acoustically;

children.label: C
children.text: uses dynamic habitat suitability models to inform the likelihood of North Atlantic right whale occurrence habitats described in paragraph (2) at any given time;

children.label: D
children.text: coordinates with the Integrated Ocean Observing System of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration and Regional Ocean Partnerships to leverage monitoring assets;

children.label: E
children.text: integrates historical data;

children.label: F
children.text: integrates new near real-time monitoring methods and technologies as such methods and technologies become available;

children.label: G
children.text: accurately verifies and rapidly communicates detection data to appropriate ocean users;

children.label: H
children.text: creates standards for contributing, and allows ocean users to contribute, data to the monitoring system using comparable near real-time monitoring methods and technologies;

children.label: I
children.text: communicates the risks of injury to large cetaceans to ocean users in a manner that is most likely to result in informed decision-making regarding the mitigation of those risks; and

children.label: J
children.text: minimizes additional stressors to large cetaceans as a result of the information available to ocean users.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Under Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives, and make available to the public, a preliminary report on the pilot project.December 23, 2022

children.label: I
children.text: A description of the monitoring methods and technology in use or planned for deployment under the pilot project.

children.label: II
children.text: An analysis of the efficacy of the methods and technology in use or planned for deployment for detecting North Atlantic right whales.

children.label: III
children.text: An assessment of the manner in which the monitoring system designed and deployed under this subsection is directly informing and improving the management, health, and survival of North Atlantic right whales.

children.label: IV
children.text: A prioritized identification of technology or research gaps.

children.label: V
children.text: A plan to communicate the risks of injury to large cetaceans to ocean users in a manner that is most likely to result in informed decision making regarding the mitigation of such risks.

children.label: VI
children.text: Any other information on the potential benefits and efficacy of the pilot project the Under Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 6 years after , the Under Secretary, in coordination with the heads of other relevant Federal agencies, shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives, and make available to the public, a final report on the pilot project.December 23, 2022

children.label: I
children.text: address the preliminary report required under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: aa
children.text: an assessment of the benefits and efficacy of the pilot project;

children.label: AA
children.text: to additional large cetaceans of concern for which such measures would reduce risk of serious injury or death; and

children.label: BB
children.text: in important feeding, breeding, calving, rearing, or migratory habitats of large cetaceans that co-occur with areas of high risk of mortality or serious injury from vessel strikes or disturbance;

children.label: cc
children.text: a budget and description of funds necessary to carry out such plan;

children.label: dd
children.text: a prioritized plan for acquisition, deployment, and maintenance of monitoring technologies; and

children.label: ee
children.text: the locations or species to which such plan would apply.

children.heading: Mitigation protocols
children.label: e Mitigation protocols
children.text: The Under Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary, the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of Transportation, and the Secretary of the Interior, and with input from affected stakeholders, shall develop and deploy mitigation protocols that make use of any monitoring system designed and deployed under this section to direct sector-specific mitigation measures that avoid and significantly reduce risk of serious injury and mortality to North Atlantic right whales.

children.heading: Access to data
children.label: f Access to data
children.text: The Under Secretary shall provide access to data generated by any monitoring system designed and deployed under this section for purposes of scientific research and evaluation and public awareness and education, including through the Right Whale Sighting Advisory System of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration and WhaleMap or other successor public website portals, subject to review for national security considerations.

children.heading: Additional authority
children.label: g Additional authority
children.text: The Under Secretary may enter into and perform such contracts, leases, grants, or cooperative agreements as may be necessary to carry out this section on such terms as the Under Secretary considers appropriate, consistent with the Federal Acquisition Regulation.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: h Savings clause
children.text: An activity may not be carried out under this section if the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Under Secretary, determines that the activity would negatively impact the defense readiness or the national security of the United States.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: i Funding
children.text: From funds otherwise appropriated to the Under Secretary $5,000,000 is authorized to support development, deployment, application, and ongoing maintenance of the Program and to otherwise carry out this section for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.
